project.name: OE Dashboard
project.description: Real-time Operational Excellence Dashboard for VietJet Air, showing flight ops, fleet, crew, weather, alerts, ground ops, and network data

data_source.sync: Python ETL scripts (separate repo) sync AIMS → Supabase

database_schema.summary: Dashboard reads from Supabase tables synced by AIMS. 5 existing tables from ETL + 3 new tables for dashboard-specific data

existing_tables.name: flights
existing_tables.key_columns: id(int PK), flight_date(date), carrier_code, flight_number(int), departure, arrival, std, sta, atd, ata, aircraft_reg, status, delay_code, delay_minutes

existing_tables.name: airports
existing_tables.key_columns: id(int PK), code, name, country_code, latitude, longitude

existing_tables.name: aircraft
existing_tables.key_columns: id(int PK), registration, aircraft_type, serial_number, country

existing_tables.name: crew_members
existing_tables.key_columns: id(int PK), crew_id, crew_name, base, email

existing_tables.name: crew_flight_hours
existing_tables.key_columns: id(int PK), crew_id, calculation_date, hours_28_day, hours_12_month, warning_level

existing_tables.name: crew_roster
existing_tables.key_columns: id(int PK), crew_id, duty_date, duty_code, flight_number

new_tables.name: alerts
new_tables.purpose: Operational alerts (AOG, crew, weather, ground, tech)
new_tables.key_columns: id(uuid PK), severity, category, flight_id(int FK), title, status

new_tables.name: weather_reports
new_tables.key_columns: id(uuid PK), airport_code, condition, temperature_c, wind_speed_kts, visibility_m, severity, sigmet_id

new_tables.name: ground_ops_metrics
new_tables.purpose: Ground handling stage times per airport
new_tables.key_columns: id(uuid PK), airport_code, stage, avg_time_minutes, status

gotchas: flights.id is INTEGER, not UUID or TEXT
flights.flight_date is DATE type, not TEXT — use CURRENT_DATE not CURRENT_DATE::TEXT
airports has no 'city' column — use 'name' instead
aircraft has no 'status' or 'utilization' columns — only registry data
Supabase has 1000-row limit per query — use .range() pagination for large datasets
Supabase in_() filter has ~300 item limit

views: DashboardView - KPIs, flight list, airport OTP, alerts
FlightDetailView - single flight deep-dive with timeline
FleetView - aircraft registry and fleet composition
NetworkView - airborne map, sectors, flight tracker
hooks: useDashboard - KPIs + today's flights + alerts + weather + airport OTP
useFleet - aircraft list + fleet summary view
useNetwork - today's flights for network map

api_endpoints.explained: All data access goes through Supabase client — no custom backend API. Tables accessed: flights, airports, aircraft, alerts, weather_reports, ground_ops_metrics, plus SQL views.

business_rules: Operational day = 04:00 local → 03:59 local next day
Flight display format: carrier_code + flight_number (e.g., VJ126)
Ground ops stages: CHECK-IN, SECURITY, BOARDING, LOADING, FUELING, CATERING, CLEANING

patterns: All hooks follow pattern: fetchData + useState + useEffect + realtime subscription
Views render LoadingSpinner/ErrorMessage for loading/error states
Dark theme with #211112 backgrounds, #db1f25 VietJet red accent
gotchas: import.meta.env needs (import.meta as any).env cast for tsc
Object.values() loses type info — use explicit Record<> type
React sub-components need React.FC typing for key prop to work
PowerShell needs -ExecutionPolicy Bypass for npm commands
conventions: File naming: PascalCase for components, camelCase for hooks
Colors: VietJet red #db1f25, dark bg #211112, emerald for success
